sections.content: World model: SPACE toggles player between [27,24] (yellow cross center, cross split into 4 arms) and top-left (cross merged). Blue L-shape stays at top-left. Player can move with arrow keys. I need to understand what happens when I move the player while at the yellow cross center - do the yellow arms move with the player?

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try moving DOWN from the yellow cross center to see if yellow arms move with player
action(['DOWN'])

nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
from collections import defaultdict
by_color = defaultdict(list)
for n in nodes:
    by_color[n['color']].append(n)

print("After DOWN from yellow center:")
for n in by_color['W']:
    print(f"  White: boundary={n['boundary']}")
for n in by_color['Y']:
    b = n['boundary']
    if b:
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if n['pixels'] > 5:
            print(f"  Yellow: bounds=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}] pixels={n['pixels']}")
        else:
            print(f"  Yellow dot: boundary={b}")
for n in by_color['b']:
    b = n['boundary']
    if b and n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  Blue: bounds=[{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}] pixels={n['pixels']}")

print(f"\nResult: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
